item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Monday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
genius
yes
a rare occurrence
what else could it mean?
don't try this on your flatscreen
as a guy... yeah, i've done this
i would die for plant doggo
wait what
dislike
i've been attacked
try the and
crazy song
real mature
that's gotta be rough for penguins
and there you have it
mmm hmm
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: I BEAT TYPE 2 DIABETES!
item.description: I'm very happy to say that in 3 months time, I beat type 2 diabetes and lost 17 Ibs in the process. I'm so excited! It's been a lot of hard work and sacrifice. I doubt this will go anywhere but downvoted but I had to share with someone ?
item.dc:creator: ValAmelia

item.title: Boxty is a traditional Irish potato pancake eaten at this time of year
item.description:
If you want to try a legit and delicious Irish recipe this season I recommend this one. https://www.leviathanfood.com/recipes/irish-boxty
If you’re into cooking, recipes and writing about craft and culture I have just made a Substack and would really love to see ya there and it would really help me out if you signed up https://leviathanfood.substack.com/ I also have a documentary on food and crafting in Ireland coming out very soon: If anyone is interested here is the trailer: https://youtu.be/AWjvo9-g7qQ?si=qdWBIksAV0VL7F4r
item.dc:creator: LeviathanFood

item.title: Since they transfer the solar heat away in the form of electricity, I guess they cool off the area, too, as well as provide much needed shade. Thoughts?

item.dc:creator: yeahhedugit

item.title: It's been a while - Cute animals.
item.description:
We can all use some cute animals these days... If there's no sound, either I never had it, or I deleted the terrible music.
To reset your birb to factory settings press down on both ears and scritch the top of the head simultaneously.
Why did the geese cross the road?
I didn't know - I keep getting ads saying there are a lot in my area...
Me too little buddy... me too.
Be not afraid.
Murder training starts early. Don't worry, he'll learn.
Crows are amazing.
Translation Request.
Big scritches!
Sound on for adorable birb silliness.
Kid... you looked back twice, then literally stood there and took it...
So many happy wiggles!
Knock Knock!
unmute for kitty purrs
Unmute for beautiful singing
Unmute for birds at the meeting - they're trying to figure out who is the Imposter. Yellow was not doing tasks.
Unmute for happy fox noises. Courtesy of SaveAFox.
Unmute for sassy husky huffs.
Unmute for belly pats.
This bike has a strange horn...
Otterly adorable
Just taking a shower... A little privacy, please?
The cutest cuddle puddle.
The chosen one.
Those wings are gorgeous!
Look at that happy smile!
Not usually a fan of the danger noodles... but I must admit he's very cute with that hat.
I opted to leave the music... because you can still hear the foxes... I am not pleased.
Unmute for adorable pitter patters.
Excuse me, I think you dropped something...
Snow IS magical!
Meep!
What a gentleman.
item.dc:creator: bobthefunny

item.title: Im not exaggerating when I say I hate whoever made this
item.dc:creator: zoraniko

item.title: What Happened Today #38
item.description: The new normal? NIH censors research, NASA guts leadership, and Trump’s admin warns 60 universities while slashing healthcare access. Musk calls a U.S. senator a traitor, a Columbia grad is jailed for his activism, and D.C. is forced to erase BLM Plaza after GOP threats. Meanwhile, the Dow crashes, and the government tightens its grip. #Resist #GovBriefToday The stakes are clear: Staying informed isn’t enough. Call your elected officials. Attend local meetings. Volunteer. Vote in every election—big or small. The only way to stop this is to show up and push back. LINKS 1. https://www.axios.com/2025/03/11/senator-mark-kelly-ukraine-trump-aid-intelligence 2. https://www.propublica.org/article/national-cancer-institute-flagged-topics-vaccines-autism-rfk-jr 3. https://www.cnn.com/2025/03/10/politics/rubio-usaid-contracts-state-department/index.html 4. https://www.nbcwashington.com/news/local/cannot-erase-our-history-black-lives-matter-plaza-honored-before-removal-begins-monday/3862736/ 5. https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/arrest-of-palestinian-activist-at-columbia-will-be-first-of-many-to-come-trump-says 6. https://www.newsweek.com/stocks-get-hammered-after-donald-trump-refuses-rule-out-recession-2042497 7. https://www.washingtonpost.com/health/2025/03/10/vaccines-nih-rfk-research-canceled/ 8. https://thehill.com/business/budget/5187286-dc-mayor-house-gop-stopgap-plan/ 9. https://www.nytimes.com/2025/03/10/health/measles-texas-kennedy-fox.html 10. https://www.mediaite.com/news/doj-official-ousted-after-refusing-to-recommend-mel-gibsons-gun-rights-be-restored/ 11. https://www.seattletimes.com/nation-world/nation-politics/judge-orders-usaid-and-state-department-to-pay-funds-unlawfully-withheld/ 12. https://www.axios.com/2025/03/10/harvard-yale-universities-funding-cuts 13. https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5187282-doge-open-records-judge-rules/ 14. https://www.reuters.com/world/us/nasa-terminates-chief-scientist-role-closes-policy-office-2025-03-10/ 15. https://www.seattletimes.com/seattle-news/law-justice/top-law-firm-targeted-by-trump-over-clinton-campaign-work-hires-top-litigators/ 16. https://www.yahoo.com/news/trump-administration-plans-limit-obamacare-201307269.html FIGHTING BACK - https://cybernews.com/news/x-outage-hacktivist-dark-storm-protest-musk-and-trump-/ Subscribe to Get Headlines Every Day or View Archives - https://govbrief.today
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: I have many good friends in Plattsburgh and Burlington, but we just voicecall for now
item.description: When I was a kid before 9/11, we crossed the border just to go shopping on the weekend. Even as an adult, I used to go to the US every two or three weekends for many years just to hang out with my friends from my college days (Mcgill U graduate), but now with everything that's going on, I'm scared to go at all. I feel like you guys probably hate me or don't wanna see me.
item.dc:creator: ChemistryAndPhysicsAreFun

item.dc:creator: tolson9

item.title: ITS MY CAKE DAY!
item.description:
Just to kick things off and lose all the conservatives, all organised religion is a scam.
Most. Metal. Band. EVAR!
For sportsing, I like to watch rugby and shout at the tv. I also swim stupidly long distances in shark-infested waters. (well, technically, anywhere in the sea has sharks in it!)
Not a day for kilts so?
How I view you lot.
Jay and Silent Bob, doling out consequences for talking shit on the interwoogie since forever.
New challenge, find the narwhal! Chateau d'If, Marseille. I am in there somewhere, the only idiot not wearing a wetsuit. I regret nothing!
Very unfair to leave water out of the band.
Pick your placename. I might stay at tonguers point for a while.
A final word. Given everything that's going on in the world, i'm just tired of it all. Aliens, I volunteer to be abducted! If people could do the most basic thing in the world and just not be cunts to each other, the planet would be a much nicer place. Peace out! Here's to the next ten years.
item.dc:creator: gobnaitOLunacy

item.title: Tesla California sales down 11.6%
item.description: https://electrek.co/2025/02/03/teslas-sales-drop-double-digits-in-the-us-biggest-ev-market-while-others-are-growing/ The US and China are Tesla's biggest markets, roughly 38% of sales each. Germany and France are about 5% each, Germany being the third biggest Tesla market and France being the fourth. California is Tesla's most important market and they seem to be losing it. https://roadgenius.com/cars/ev/statistics/sales-by-automaker/tesla/
item.dc:creator: Apeofdeath

item.dc:creator: Othagovna

item.title: Saddle up for a new restoration!
item.description:
Well, now that things seem to have calmed down with my old kitty Story, I thought it'd be a good time to finally drag this mechanical nightmare onto the workbench and get to work! This is a 1913 crank register that I bought from a local seller over in Wausau, Wisconsin a couple of months ago for all of $400. Because I had just finished work on a larger machine and wanted to give my shoulders some time to actually recover from wrestling with it, I offered to pay the guy another $100 if he'd drive it to my house and carry it to my bench. So, on a bitterly cold and icy night, this brass beast landed in my house and promptly sat there doing nothing while I spent some time writing, playing Minecraft, and trying to keep my cat from imploding. What a winter. ><
So anyway, this weekend I set aside some time to assess what I had gotten my grubby fingers on and see what kind of mess I was in for this time. Well, at first blush, it looks like a lot of cleaning. This thing isn't just filthy with tarnish, it's filthy with decades of caked on grime that has a distinctly unpleasant smell to it. My assumption at the outset was that it was home to at least one rodent, which I'd eventually confirm. Don't worry, no mummified Mickeys in this project. The other issue I can see is that the white oak cash box is so dried out that it feels rough to the touch. I'm not sure if it's dry rotted or just needs oil and a sealant, but what I can see isn't reassuring. The more I look at it, the more I find. Which, for a 112 year old machine, is to be expected.
The grime us everywhere, and unfortunately it's also pretty tacky to the touch. All of the stippling cast into the brass is smoothed over with it, which makes me think there's moisture being trapped against the metal. The last register I had this issue on, I lost a lot of brass just from what flaked off when I washed it with soap. I'm hoping this isn't a replay of that machine, but if it is then I'll be ready for it.
I'm not sure what's caused the discoloration on the drawer front, but as soon as I saw that dry patch where the varnish is worn through, I knew I'd have sanding in my immediate future. If so, the goal will be to remove just enough surface wood to give me a better foundation to stain, oil, and finish. I want to keep the battlescars like that chipped corner, not the stains from someone's Coke spill. ;p
Did I mention it's filthy? Get on in there. Lookit the grime. That's peak antiquery right there, ah-yup. Worrying aside, those keys are in great condition. I'll be super excited to see how they pop once they're cleaned off. -- Also, for the Antiques Roadshow watchers out there, cleaning this register isn't going to destroy its value. These were business machines made by the millions, not Rembrandt originals. Restoring them extends their lifespan and adds value because, hey, they don't smell like a barn anymore. ;)
Checking the drawer brought some surprises I didn't expect! Usually I find a key, or a rat's nest. Mostly they're already empty. This one is of a different caliber, nyuck nyuck.
So, a few bullets. Pew pew. We're in deer hunting territory, so nothing too surprising. These got donated to the police station, where they dispose of loose ammunition for free. Better than tossing them in the trash and risking them popping off in the compactor truck.
I'm not one hundred percent sure where this is from. Definitely a printer plate for a receipt roller, but if it's from this register I haven't found its home. It's very soft metal, probably has some lead in it, so I'm setting it aside until I know more.
Now this was interesting. At first I thought this was the roller that went to that brass plate, but upon closer inspection I could see there's lettering already buried under that utter -heap- of oxidized gunk. Before diving into disassembly on the register itself, I wanted to see if I could clean this off enough to read what it used to say.
So, here's a snippet of what that cleanup looks like. Some of you keep saying you want more video of the process, so here's an attempt at making a very dull process entertaining. ;p This took me closer to fifteen minutes of spraying on alcohol to loose ln the old ink, using a dental wax cutter to pick material out of the crevices in the font, and clear away the excess with a nylon brush. Rinse, repeat, and repeat again.
And here's what it ended up looking like!
I wasn't sure what this engraving was supposed to be, but I thought I was seeing shirt buttons.
I cracked open a pen and schmutzed some ink on the roller, and here's the image. It's still rough, but you can make out a woman on and old timey candlestick telephone with the caption: "Phone Orders Receive Prompt and Careful Attention. Only One Quality, THE BEST. (ever)" Will modern technology never cease to amaze? :)
Well, it's a getting late and I'm getting dozey! I would have included the disassembly, but Imgur was being fickle and I ran out of time. I'll have another update with the full disassembly soon! Until then! (Also, real quick, I have a Ko-fi for anyone who might like to support my hobby! It tends to eat up nitrile gloves and shop towels like candy, not to mention buffing compound and degreaser. If you want to chip in, I'm over here: Ko-fi.com/elbowdeepinahorse)
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInAHorse
